canonical_or_religious_significance: One of most important tirthas in Jainism
historical_significance: Revered Jain pilgrimage town in Hassan district of Karnataka. Famous for 58-foot monolithic statue of Lord Bahubali (Gomateshwara) one of tallest free-standing statues in world. Site reached peak in architectural and sculptural activity under patronage of Western Ganga dynasty. According to Digambara Jain tradition, connection to Jainism in region attributed to Mauryan Emperor Chandragupta Maurya who is believed to have come here on pilgrimage having received diksha from Acharya Bhadrabahu. Digambara sources state Chandragupta Basadi originally built by Ashoka in third century BCE. Town located between twin hills Vindhyagiri and Chandragiri. Main statue of Bahubali carved out of single block of granite located atop Vindhyagiri hill. Mahamastakabhisheka grand consecration ceremony performed once every 12 years where statue bathed with milk, curds, ghee, saffron and gold coins attracting devotees from around world. Chandragiri hill has 14 different Jain temples including Shantinatha Basadi, Parshvanatha Basadi, Bhadrabahu Basadi and Chandragupta Basadi. Historical accounts differ between Digambara and Svetambara traditions regarding Chandragupta connection, with Svetambara texts placing Bhadrabahu near Himalayan foothills.

Located in Moodabidri town in Dakshina Kannada district of Karnataka. Moodabidri occasionally referred to as Jaina Kashi of South India. In all 18 Jain temples at Moodabidri with most situated near Jain Matha. Main temple Tribhuvana Tilaka Chudamani also called 1008 pillars temple. Construction completed 29 September 1430. Built completely of stone with outer surface covered completely with copper. Temple constructed with 1008 pillars hence called 1008 pillars temple or Savira Kambada Basadi in Kannada. Houses panchaloha (5 metals) idol of Bhagawan Chandranatha in kayotsarga posture. Pillars intricately carved no two pillars alike. Important pilgrimage site for Jains in South India.

Chief Jain centre in Udupi district of Karnataka. Known for monolithic idol of Lord Bahubali along with 18 Digambar Jain temples, Jain Math and rich Jain heritage. Located 35 km from Udupi and 52 km from Mangalore. Monolithic statue installed during period 1455-1460 by ruler Veera Pandya Deva Raya. He became popular as Abhinava Pandya. Statue attractively carved throughout its length. Has Manastambha with mantapa at top containing Tirthankar idols facing four sides. Site has Chaturmukha Basadi built 1586 AD by ruler Virabhadra Raya dedicated to Lord Adinatha with 108 intricately carved pillars. Temple has four identical entrances in all four cardinal directions symbolizing openness and universal accessibility. Kere Basadi built 1545 AD by Pandyapodeya located in middle of pond has idol of Lord Adinath. Hire Basadi built 1390-1420 AD by King Vera Byaraarasa has black colored idol of Lord Neminath.

Located in North Arcot district of Tamil Nadu 51 km from Tiruvannamalai and 17 km north-east of Polur. Jain site has more than 2000 years of history. Dating back to at least 9th century Tirumalai important Jain center since ancient times. Complex includes 3 Jain caves, 2 Jain temples and 16.25-foot-high sculpture of Tirthankara Neminatha thought to date from 12th century that is tallest Jain image in Tamil Nadu. Main temple built early 11th century CE by Chola princess Kundavai. Unique Jain temple modelled on Chola temples with Raja Gopuram, Mukha mandapa, Ardha mandapa, Antharala and Vimana with Kalasha over Garbhagriha all sculptured with Jain motifs. Temple renovated and supported by Vijayanagara kings. Temple situated on top of Tirumulai hill with engraving of Mahavira flanked by two lions. Temple partly excavated and partly built within caverons of rock rich in sculptures and base-relief. Footprints of four great Jain saints Vrishabhsenachrya, Samanatabhadracharya, Varadattachrya munivar and Sri Vadeepa Simhasuri present.

Group of temples in Humcha village of Shimoga district in Karnataka. Constructed 7th century CE during period of Santara dynasty. Regarded as one of major Jain centres of Karnataka. Padmavati Basadi most well-known of these temples. According to tradition, Jinadatta Raya was Jain prince from Mathura who came from Northern India with idol of Devi Padmavati and founded kingdom in Humcha establishing seat of Bhattaraka in 7th-8th century CE. Current structure of Padmavati temple built by Vira Santara in 1061 CE. Santharas founded by Jinadatta Raya split into two branches one in Kalasa and other in Shimoga district. Santharas built many Jain temples and were main people to spread Jainism in region. Humcha Jain Matha important religious institution. Vikrama Santara built Guddada Basadi dedicated to Bahubali in 897 CE and Parshvanath temple in 950 CE.

Historic Jain temple located in Gerusoppa small village in Honavar taluk of Uttara Kannada district in Karnataka. Believed to have been built in 12th century AD. 14th century Jain basti one of Karnataka's architectural wonders hidden in midst of Western Ghats in Uttar Kannada district. Built completely out of granite. Contains statues of four Jain tirthankaras. Temple complex said to have been built by Chennabairadevi seems incomplete. Reign of Chennabairadevi the Pepper Queen referred to as golden age of Salva Dynasty marked by export of high quality pepper grown in nearby areas. Gerusoppa erstwhile capital of Saluvas. Ancient Jain heritage site. Journey takes past mountain waterfalls and across perilous rope bridges that hang over Sharavati river. Dedicated to Jain tirthankaras or spiritual leaders.

Jain temple located in Varanga village in Udupi district of Karnataka. Ancient Digambar Jain temple in Hebri taluk. Located 37 km from Udupi and 8.6 km from Hebri. Star-shaped temple built in 12th century. Temple set in middle of 14-acre reservoir hence called Kere Basadi (Lake Temple). Features intricate carvings and sculptures exemplifying ancient craftsmanship. Temple houses idols of Parswanatha, Ananthanath, Neminatha and Shantinatha Tirthankaras on single pedestal with faces directed towards four distinct cardinal directions symbolizing Jain philosophy. Varanga said to be one of oldest Jain temples in area with long and ancient history dating 800 to 1000 years old. Varanga has 3 Digambar Jain temples: Matada Basadi, Kere Basadi/Chaturmukha Basadi and Neminath Basadi/Hire Basadi. Village has over 20 Jain inscriptions. One of very few ancient Chaturmukha Jain temples of world.

Located in Kolanupaka village in Nalgonda district of Telangana. Jain temple of Mahavira situated 83 km from Warangal and 81 km from Hyderabad. Temple decorated with beautiful images of Tirthankaras. One of important Jain pilgrimage sites in country. According to legendary tradition, main temple said to have been built by Emperor Bharata son of King Dushyanta and Queen Sakuntala. Jainism prevalent in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana before 4th century and Kolanupaka one of prominent centers of Jainism from early times. About 20 Jain inscriptions found. Kolanupaka flourished as Jain center during Rashtrakutas period. Architecture of temple stunning with wonderful carvings on temple walls. Temple made using red sandstone and pillars made of white marble. Temple was in ruined condition for years renovated in late 20th century employing more than 150 artisans from Rajasthan and Gujarat. Old garbhagruha preserved and complete new temple created surrounding existing tower. Lord Mahaveer statue 130 cm tall made from single piece of jade. Mata Padmavati and Lord Simandhar placed on both sides. Temple centered on dharamshala known as Someshwara built by Chalukyas in 800s. Inscriptions date from 12th century mention temple activity.

Located in Dharmasthala one of important pilgrim centers of Karnataka. Town known for religious tolerance. Chandranathaswami Basadi Jain temple located nearby Nelyadi Beedu beside Car Museum. Dharmasthala houses Hindu Manjunathaswami Temple major attraction and Jain Basadi dedicated to Chandranatha Swamy 8th Jain Tirthankara along with magnificent statue of Bahubali/Gomateshwara son of first Jain Tirthankara Rishabadeva. Place makes it site of worship for both Jains and Hindus. Important pilgrimage destination in Karnataka reflecting syncretic religious traditions.

Located in Belgaum district of Karnataka. Important Jain pilgrimage center in North Karnataka. Kamala Basti main Jain temple in city. Region has rich Jain heritage with several ancient temples. Important center for Jain community in Karnataka.
